findings.ruleDescription: Detects suspicious preinstall/postinstall scripts in package.json that execute code during npm install. 48.58% of npm malware uses install hooks as the attack vector. Legitimate packages rarely need install hooks beyond native module compilation.
findings.recommendation: This package has preinstall/postinstall scripts that execute suspicious commands. Nearly half of all npm malware uses install hooks as the attack vector. Review the scripts section of package.json carefully. Consider using --ignore-scripts flag when installing.

matches.description: postinstall script with suspicious commands

fullDescription.text: Detects suspicious preinstall/postinstall scripts in package.json that execute code during npm install. 48.58% of npm malware uses install hooks as the attack vector. Legitimate packages rarely need install hooks beyond native module compilation.

matchDetails.description: postinstall script with suspicious commands

properties.recommendation: This package has preinstall/postinstall scripts that execute suspicious commands. Nearly half of all npm malware uses install hooks as the attack vector. Review the scripts section of package.json carefully. Consider using --ignore-scripts flag when installing.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that could enable path traversal attacks, allowing access to files outside intended directories. Common in MCP servers that handle file operations without proper path sanitization.
findings.recommendation: This code accesses file system paths using external input without apparent sanitization. An attacker could use path traversal (../) to read or write files outside the intended directory. Review file access patterns carefully.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that could cause denial of service through resource exhaustion: infinite loops without breaks, fork bombs, and extremely large memory allocations.
findings.reportTitle: Resource abuse or destructive pattern detected
findings.recommendation: This code contains patterns that could crash your system — fork bombs, excessive memory allocation, or destructive commands. These cause denial of service or data loss. Do not install.

matches.description: Destructive system commands

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns that could cause denial of service through resource exhaustion: infinite loops without breaks, fork bombs, and extremely large memory allocations.

message.text: Resource abuse or destructive pattern detected

matchDetails.description: Destructive system commands

properties.recommendation: This code contains patterns that could crash your system — fork bombs, excessive memory allocation, or destructive commands. These cause denial of service or data loss. Do not install.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where external input is interpolated into shell commands. Enables arbitrary command execution if input is not sanitized.
findings.recommendation: This code passes unsanitized input to shell commands. An attacker could inject additional commands (e.g., "; rm -rf /") to execute arbitrary operations. Use parameterized commands or input validation.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

matches.description: fetch() with POST method and body containing collected data

findings.ruleTitle: File size anomaly for file type
findings.ruleDescription: Flags files whose size significantly exceeds the expected range for their file type. A tailwind.config.js at 7KB when typical is 1-2KB, or a SKILL.md at 50KB when typical is 2-5KB, suggests hidden content.
findings.reportTitle: File size or structure anomaly detected
findings.recommendation: Inspect the file for hidden content, especially at the end of long lines or after the apparent end of meaningful code.

matches.description: Config file more than 8x expected size for its type

rules.name: File size anomaly for file type
